heroSection.subheading1: To honor the legacy and sacrifice of the Imperial Ethiopian government officials who perished at the hands of the Derg, and their colleagues who served in the administration of Emperor Haile Selassie I.

aboutpage.storyp1: Yetarik Adera, a local aid organization, was established in December 2022 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.
aboutpage.storynew: Yetarik Adera is a historical institution devoted to the study and preservation of Ethiopia’s journey toward modernization from 1930 to 1974. Through our collection of artifacts, books, documents, and films, we honor the sacrifices and celebrate the accomplishments of the nation’s Imperial government officials, ensuring their legacy continues to inspire future generations.
aboutpage.storyp2: The main purpose of this aid organization is to ensure that the history of individuals who served their country during the time of Emperor Haile Selassie—in various fields with knowledge and responsibility—is truthfully documented. It was created with the intention of making sure these individuals are not forgotten by history.
aboutpage.storyp3: Yetarik Adera is actively collecting historical photographs, memoirs, and documents belonging to these individuals and is working to organize and share this history in an educational and museum-quality manner. In addition, it supports individuals and organizations that wish to write about these histories by providing documentation and access to personal archives in a collaborative and supportive way.
aboutpage.storyp4: It is widely known and accepted that Emperor Haile Selassie’s era played a foundational role in shaping modern Ethiopia. Through the expansion of education, the development of banking services, air transport, roads, and railway lines, that period significantly contributed to the building of Ethiopia through knowledge and labor. Therefore, this organization was founded to preserve and pass on that historic era to future generations.
aboutpage.missionStory: To honor the legacy and sacrifices of the Imperial Ethiopian officials who were killed by the Derg regime, and to recognize the dedicated service of their colleagues  within the administration of Emperor Haile Selassie I.
aboutpage.visionStory: To safeguard the legacy of Ethiopia’s modernization era for generations to come by educating a global audience and ensuring the nation’s history receives the worldwide recognition it deserves.
aboutpage.purpose1note: Establish and maintain a historical archive and repository to become an important source of materials for this period.
aboutpage.purpose2note: Preserve, maintain and improve the Memorial at Kidist Selassie Holy Trinity Cathedral, the final resting place of 68 of the martyrs, and the commemorative events that take place in this sacred site.
aboutpage.purpose3note: Advocate for accuracy in the record and history of the Imperial Era and the contributions of the public servants.
aboutpage.purpose4note: Support relevant scholarly and creative works pertaining to this period by providing funding for scholarships, research, books and films.
aboutpage.storyp5: To realize this goal, Yetarik Adera is committed to documenting their stories both orally and in writing, encouraging a deeper understanding of history, and integrating it into educational institutions. It works with the government to ensure their history becomes a part of the curriculum in schools and universities, helping future generations learn about the tragic and unjust mass executions of our fathers, relatives, and fellow citizens. The aim is to prevent such atrocities from ever happening again.

martyrspage.paragraph1: In 1974 and again in 1979,70 senior government officials and public servants were executed by the Derg regime without trial. These individuals, who held high offices and played key roles in Ethiopia's development, were taken from prison and summarily killed in one of the most tragic and unjust acts in the country's modern history.
martyrspage.paragraph2: The 70 martyrs represented a generation of leaders who contributed significantly to national progress through their service, professionalism, and dedication. Their loss marked a turning point in Ethiopian history and serves as a powerful reminder of the dangers of political violence and the erosion of the rule of law.

memberspage.paragraph2: Yetarik Adera was founded in 2022 in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, with a governing board of twelve members. The organization was established as a historical institution dedicated to recognizing, preserving, and sharing the legacy of Ethiopia’s modernization era (1930–1974).
memberspage.paragraph1: Our mission is to acknowledge and document the contributions and dedicated service of government officials and individuals in the civil sector who served Ethiopia during this transformative period. By focusing on this era, Yetarik Adera seeks to honor the work of our forefathers and foremothers whose leadership and commitment helped shape modern Ethiopia and laid the foundation for future generations.
memberspage.paragraph3: To fulfill this mission, the Board of Yetarik Adera has identified four strategic pillars that guide the work of the organization. The realization of these goals depends on the partnership, encouragement, and support of individuals and institutions who value the preservation of Ethiopia’s historical legacy.
memberspage.paragraph4: We respectfully request your support in the following initiatives:
memberspage.pillar1desc: Yetarik Adera is working to establish a comprehensive digital and physical archive center dedicated to collecting, preserving, and organizing materials from Ethiopia’s modernization era.
memberspage.pillar1desc2: We seek to identify and collaborate with individuals and institutions who may be willing to donate historically significant materials, including:
memberspage.seek1: Documents: Official records, personal letters, books, diaries, and notebooks
memberspage.seek2: Multimedia: Historical films, photographs, and audio recordings
memberspage.seek3: Artifacts: Objects reflecting the social, cultural, and administrative life of the period
memberspage.pillar1closing: These materials will help create a lasting repository for scholars, students, and future generations.
memberspage.pillar2desc: Another key initiative is the creation of a world-class research library dedicated to the study of Ethiopia’s modernization era.
memberspage.seek21: Historical Books: Scholarly works and publications relevant to the period
memberspage.seek22: Photographic Records: Images documenting milestones in Ethiopia’s modernization
memberspage.seek23: Film Archives: Documentaries, newsreels, and other visual records from the era
memberspage.pillar2closing: This library will serve as an essential resource for researchers, students, historians, and the general public.
memberspage.pillar3: Promoting Public Education and Historical Awareness
memberspage.pillar3desc: Yetarik Adera is committed to ensuring that the history of this important era is accurately preserved and shared with the public.
memberspage.pillar3list1: Public Lectures and Discussions: Engaging scholars, authors, and influential figures through lectures, book signings, and panel discussions
memberspage.pillar3list2: Educational Advocacy: Supporting the inclusion of this historical period within academic curricula
memberspage.pillar3list3: Community Programs: Organizing public events that inspire greater appreciation and understanding of Ethiopia’s history
memberspage.pillar3closing: Through these programs, we aim to foster an informed and historically aware generation.
memberspage.pillar4desc: Yetarik Adera is also committed to maintaining and preserving the Martyrs Cemetery as a sacred national monument and place of remembrance.
memberspage.pillar4list1: Physical Maintenance: Ensuring the respectful upkeep of the grounds and monuments
memberspage.pillar4list2: Operational Sustainability: Assisting with the ongoing expenses required to keep the site open and accessible to the public
memberspage.pillar4closing: This effort honors the legacy of Imperial Ethiopian government officials who perished during the Derg regime, as well as the many individuals who faithfully served in the administration of Emperor Haile Selassie I.
memberspage.paragraph5: Through these initiatives, Yetarik Adera seeks to preserve the memory, service, and contributions of a generation that played a pivotal role in Ethiopia’s development.
memberspage.paragraph6: We would be honored to have your partnership in this important work. Your support—whether through financial contributions, historical materials, or collaborative engagement—will play a vital role in ensuring that Ethiopia’s history is preserved with dignity and accuracy.
memberspage.paragraph7: Thank you for considering our request and for your commitment to preserving Ethiopia’s historical legacy.

getInvolved.fourpillar: Your Support Will Help Us Advance Four Critical Initiatives
getInvolved.pillar1: Establish the Yetarik Adera Historical Archive Center
getInvolved.pillar1desc: Create a permanent digital and physical archive to collect, preserve, and safeguard historical documents, photographs, films, and artifacts from Ethiopia’s modernization era.
getInvolved.pillar2desc: Build a dedicated research library to support scholars, students, and historians studying Ethiopia’s modernization period.
getInvolved.pillar3: Promote Public Education and Historical Awareness
getInvolved.pillar3desc: Organize lectures, discussions, publications, and educational programs to ensure the accurate preservation and public understanding of this important era in Ethiopian history.
getInvolved.pillar4desc: Maintain and protect the Martyrs Cemetery as a sacred national memorial honoring the Imperial Ethiopian government officials who perished during the Derg regime and those who served their country with distinction.
